description: A vertical, portrait-orientation slogan graphic from USCIS rendered in a stylized blue-and-white duotone (cyanotype-like) treatment. The background photograph shows an arrest/processing scene: at left a person in a white shirt has their wrists being held/cuffed behind their back by an officer; a uniformed officer in dark tactical clothing stands center; at right a hand holds up a clear plastic evidence bag labeled 'PROPERTY' containing items. A large semi-transparent DHS/government circular seal (the words 'DEPARTMENT' and partial 'HOMELAND'/'COMMERCE'-style ring visible) is overlaid across the lower half as a watermark. In the lower-left corner sits a solid dark-blue rectangle containing the slogan in bold white serif text on three lines: 'Making America Safe Again.' The aesthetic is a recurring USCIS/DHS template (heavy blue tint, seal watermark, blue slogan box) used across multiple posts to brand enforcement actions. The messaging technique pairs imagery of a detained individual with the 'Making America Safe Again' slogan (a riff on Trump's MAGA branding) to portray immigration enforcement as public-safety protection. Target audience is the general public and Trump's base, signaling tough-on-crime/immigration enforcement.
